title: lupinus microcarpus var. scopulorum
intro: Lupinus microcarpus var. scopulorum, also known as the Rocky Mountain lupine, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the lupine family and is native to the western United States. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and providing antioxidant properties, which can help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Lupinus microcarpus var. scopulorum can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
